input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with a specific structure. Let me provide the code directly since this is a straightforward request for a code example.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout menu with:
   - "Dashboard" section (single page)
   - "Products" section with two tabs: "Active" and "Archived"
   - "Settings" page
2. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="My MAUI App">

    <!-- ─── Dashboard (single page, own flyout item) ─── -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- ─── Products (flyout item with two tabs) ─── -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                Icon="active.png"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                Icon="archived.png"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- ─── Settings ─── -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's a summary of the structure:

**Visual hierarchy (flyout)**
| Flyout Item | Content |
|---|---|
| Dashboard | Single `ShellContent` → `DashboardPage` |
| Products | One `Tab` with two `ShellContent` children → bottom tabs for **Active** / **Archived** |
| Settings | Single `ShellContent` → `SettingsPage` |

**Key points:**

- **`FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"`** — enables the hamburger menu.
- **`Tab` wrapper** inside the Products `FlyoutItem` gives you bottom tabs for Active/Archived on that screen.
- **`ContentTemplate` + `DataTemplate`** — pages are lazily created on first visit.
- **`Routing.RegisterRoute`** in the code-behind registers `ProductDetailsPage` for programmatic navigation since it's *not* in the visual tree.

**Navigate to details** from either products page:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"product-details?id={product.Id}");
```

Receive the parameter in `ProductDetailsPage` with `[QueryProperty]` or `IQueryAttributable`.
